metadata.summary: Mobile-first responsive design: base styles unconditional, min-width queries layer up. Breakpoints from content. clamp() for fluid typography. Container queries decouple components from viewport.

content.title: Mobile-First Responsive Design
content.summary: Mobile-first responsive design: base styles are unconditional (mobile), min-width queries layer up for larger screens. Breakpoints are derived from content, not device names. clamp() replaces stepwise media queries for typography and spacing. Container queries (@container) replace viewport queries inside components. Touch targets meet WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 44x44 CSS px.

why.p: Desktop-first CSS requires complex max-width overrides that accumulate bugs on every layout change. clamp() fluid typography eliminates 30-50% of media queries. Container queries decouple component layout from viewport size, making components portable across different page regions. Agents routinely break mobile by lifting breakpoint values from Bootstrap or omitting container-type: inline-size.

list.item: Greenfield site/app — set viewport meta, mobile-first base CSS, container queries from the first commit.
Auditing an existing layout for breakpoint regressions, touch-target sizes, and CLS on small screens.
Migrating from viewport-based to container-query-based responsive design.
Configuring Tailwind breakpoints to match real content breakpoints, not device widths.
App is locked to a single device class (kiosk, tablet-only internal tool) — fixed layout is simpler.
Framework already enforces mobile-first and the site passes Lighthouse mobile — adding breakpoints is busywork.

section.name: mobile-first-order
section.p: Write base (mobile) styles without any media query. Add min-width queries to layer desktop enhancements. Never use max-width first, as desktop-first CSS leaks desktop assumptions into the base.

rule.statement: Write base (mobile) styles without any media query. Add min-width queries to layer desktop enhancements. Never use max-width first.
rule.rationale: Desktop-first CSS leaks desktop assumptions into the base; every mobile breakpoint becomes an override that accumulates complexity.

example.title: Mobile-first grid with min-width enhancement

section.name: viewport-meta
section.p: Always set the viewport meta tag correctly. Never add maximum-scale=1 or user-scalable=no, as these violate WCAG 1.4.4.

rule.statement: Always set <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">. Never add maximum-scale=1 or user-scalable=no — this violates WCAG 1.4.4.
rule.rationale: user-scalable=no prevents users with low vision from zooming the page; it silently breaks a11y compliance.

section.name: fluid-typography-with-clamp
section.p: Use CSS clamp(min, fluid, max) for typography and spacing instead of breakpoint steps. This eliminates 30-50% of media queries and produces smooth scaling without discrete jumps.

rule.statement: Use CSS clamp(min, fluid, max) for typography and spacing instead of breakpoint steps. This eliminates 30-50% of media queries.
rule.rationale: clamp() produces a smooth scale between the min and max without discrete jumps; it works on any viewport without additional breakpoints.

example.title: Fluid typography and spacing with clamp

section.name: responsive-images
section.p: Provide srcset with multiple widths and a sizes attribute that matches the actual CSS layout. Never set sizes="100vw" for multi-column layouts.

rule.statement: Provide srcset with multiple widths and a sizes attribute that matches the actual CSS layout. Never set sizes="100vw" for multi-column layouts.
rule.rationale: Wrong sizes="100vw" on a 3-column layout causes the browser to download the largest image at every viewport, wasting mobile bandwidth.

section.name: container-queries
section.p: Container queries decouple component layout from viewport size. Set container-type: inline-size on the parent element before writing @container rules. Without it, queries silently never match.

rule.statement: Set container-type: inline-size on the parent element before writing @container rules. Without it, queries silently never match.
rule.rationale: Agents regularly write @container rules without the container-type declaration, mark the task done, and the styles never apply.

example.title: Container queries with inline-size type

section.name: touch-targets
section.p: Every interactive element (button, link, icon button) must have a minimum touch target of 44x44 CSS px per WCAG 2.5.8. This is the most common a11y regression on mobile.

rule.statement: Every interactive element (button, link, icon button) must have a minimum touch target of 44x44 CSS px per WCAG 2.5.8.
rule.rationale: Icon buttons with padding: 4px are visually compact but inaccessible on touch devices; this is the most common a11y regression on mobile.

example.title: Touch target floor for all interactive elements

section.name: ios-safari-dvh-fix
section.p: Use 100dvh for full-height layouts with a 100vh fallback. iOS Safari's 100vh includes the URL bar, causing layout jumps when it hides.

rule.statement: Use 100dvh for full-height layouts with a 100vh fallback. iOS Safari's 100vh includes the URL bar, causing layout jumps when it hides.
rule.rationale: dvh accounts for the dynamic viewport height; older browsers fall back to vh without any styling breakage.

section.name: antipatterns
section.p: Common responsive design mistakes that agents introduce when making layouts responsive.

antipattern.description: Desktop-first CSS with max-width media queries as the primary pattern.
antipattern.reason: max-width-first requires complex overrides for every mobile breakpoint; each override adds specificity that defeats future fixes.

antipattern.description: Lifting breakpoint values from Bootstrap (576/768/992/1200px) without checking the project's design tokens.
antipattern.reason: Bootstrap widths are device-sized; content-driven breakpoints (when the layout actually breaks) produce simpler CSS and fewer edge cases.

antipattern.description: Adding container-query rules without container-type: inline-size on the parent element.
antipattern.reason: Without container-type, @container rules are parsed but never match; agents check "rendered without error" and consider the task complete.

antipattern.description: Adding flex-wrap: wrap to every flex container when asked to "make it responsive".
antipattern.reason: flex-wrap on every container looks correct on phones and produces broken desktop layouts where items wrap prematurely.

antipattern.description: Setting meta name="viewport" content="user-scalable=no" or maximum-scale=1.
antipattern.reason: These attributes prevent zoom for users with low vision and fail WCAG 1.4.4. They are copied from legacy templates and are a silent a11y violation.

antipattern.description: Converting vh to dvh without keeping the vh fallback line.
antipattern.reason: Older browsers (Safari <15.4, Chrome <108) do not support dvh; removing the vh fallback breaks full-height layouts on those browsers.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Run a subagent that inventories fixed-width and min-width values in CSS, regenerates them from a tokenized breakpoint set, and snapshots the page at mobile/tablet/desktop with Playwright plus visual diff. Use Lighthouse mobile and Chrome DevTools device emulation as oracles.

rule.statement: When responsive work ties into a larger redesign, use /faion for page-by-page audit and fix loops with visual-test gates, or faion-sdd-executor-agent when explicit acceptance criteria exist. Do NOT pick breakpoints from device sizes; pin them to content (e.g., "two-column when card row exceeds 640px").
rule.rationale: Content-driven breakpoints produce maintainable CSS. Device breakpoints (iPhone 12 is 390px, iPad is 768px) become stale when devices change and force unnecessary refactoring.

list.item: Mobile-first: base styles unconditional, min-width queries layer up. Never max-width first — it leaks desktop assumptions.
Pick breakpoints from content, not device. Common content-driven set: 480 / 640 / 768 / 1024 / 1280.
Use clamp(min, fluid, max) for typography and spacing instead of stepwise breakpoints — drops 30-50% of media queries.
Always set <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1"> and never maximum-scale=1 (a11y).
Ship srcset + sizes for hero images; verify the right candidate fires at each viewport via DevTools "Resources" panel.
Test on a real low-end Android (Moto G class) at least once per release — emulators hide jank.

template.purpose: Playwright config extract with mobile-chrome, mobile-safari, tablet, desktop projects.
